question: What should I expect on my first visit?

children.text: We'll greet you at reception, walk you through the space, and have a short chat about your goals before your first session. Plan to arrive 10 to 15 minutes early so there's no rush — and wear something comfortable.

Comfortable, loose-fitting clothing in natural fibres is ideal. Avoid heavy synthetic fabrics for HBOT sessions. A changing space and lockers are provided, so you can come straight from work or training.

question: Can I combine services in one visit?

children.text: Yes — in fact, it's how many regulars use the studio. HBOT pairs naturally with a Red Light or PEMF session either before or after. Just select 'Recovery Stack' when you book, or message us and we'll build the visit around your schedule.

question: How often should I do HBOT?

children.text: There's no single answer — it depends on your goals. Many users start with one to three sessions per week and adjust based on how they feel. We'll help you figure it out during your free consultation.

Our chambers are fully transparent, well-lit and spacious enough to lie back comfortably. Many users with mild claustrophobia find them very manageable — you can see out in every direction and a team member is always within arm's reach. If you're unsure, book a free walkthrough first.

question: What does the pressure feel like on my ears?

children.text: Similar to the feeling you get on a short-haul flight. We introduce and release pressure gradually, and you'll use simple techniques (swallowing, yawning, a gentle Valsalva) to equalise. We take it at your pace — no one is ever rushed through compression.

question: Do I need medical clearance before HBOT?

children.text: Before your first session we send a short health questionnaire. Most people are cleared within minutes. Certain conditions — recent ear or sinus surgery, some medications, pregnancy — require a brief pre-screening chat or a note from your GP. We'll guide you through it.

question: Is red light safe for my eyes?

children.text: We provide protective goggles for every session and ask that you don't stare directly at the panel. With those two precautions in place, red and near-infrared light is generally considered very safe for the eye area.

question: Do I need to remove makeup or skincare first?

children.text: For the best results we recommend exposing clean, bare skin on the areas you're targeting. Heavy creams, SPF or makeup can reduce how much light reaches the skin. A quick rinse before your session is usually plenty.

question: What does a PEMF session feel like?

children.text: Most people feel almost nothing physically — the field is non-thermal and silent. Many users describe a deep sense of relaxation within a few minutes, and some fall asleep entirely. That's the intended effect.

Every 'Book' button on the site takes you to our scheduler, where you can choose a service, pick a time and pay in one flow. A confirmation email lands immediately — that's how you know your slot is held.
Reschedule or cancel up to 24 hours before your session at no charge. Late cancellations and no-shows are charged at the full session rate, so we can keep the room available for everyone.

question: Do you offer a free consultation?

children.text: Yes — a free 15-minute consultation is the best place to start if you're new. We'll walk you through the space, answer questions, and help you pick the right modality (or combination) for your goals. Select 'Free consult' when you book.

question: Should I check with my GP first?

children.text: Yes — particularly if you have any pre-existing condition, are pregnant, or take regular medication. The information on this site is educational, not a substitute for medical advice. When in doubt, ask your doctor and we'll work with whatever guidance they give you.
